rationale.R1.2: The excerpt chains several inferential steps (miscalibration → Bayesian approximation cost → verbalized-confidence alternative → RadCalib → novelty claim); logical coherence across those links is a natural target of any general review.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are present (ECE 5.7% / 8.3%, sensitivity 94.1%, specificity 91.6%, radiologist comparison) whose backing by the described experimental setup must be checkable.
rationale.R1.4: The phrase 'first verbalized-confidence model evaluated on volumetric neuroimaging' is a load-bearing novelty claim; any review naturally interrogates whether that delta is genuinely established.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look' is colloquially the most common invitation for prose-level feedback; sentence quality, hedging language, and paragraph transitions are fair game under this reading.
rationale.R1.7: Four named citations (Guo 2017, Gal & Ghahramani 2016, Lakshminarayanan 2017, Halabi 2021) with attributed numerical results make citation accuracy a live concern.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary intent a prose/writing review (sentence clarity, hedging, flow), or a substantive scientific review (verifying evidence-claim alignment, the 'first' novelty claim, and citation accuracy)?
